leadText: The Big Nugget (Japanese: でかいきんのたま Giant Gold Orb) is a valuable item introduced in Generation V. It is a larger and more valuable version of the Nugget.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] The Big Nugget serves no practical purpose other than to be sold.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description B W B2 W2 A big nugget of pure gold that gives off a lustrous gleam. A maniac will buy it for a high price. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA S V ZA A big nugget of pure gold that gives off a lustrous gleam. It can be sold at a high price to shops.

sections.title: In the core series games
sections.text: Contents 1 In the core series games 1.1 Price 1.2 Effect 1.3 Description 1.4 Acquisition 1.4.1 Distribution 1.4.1.1 As a held item In the core series games [ edit | edit source ] Price [ edit | edit source ] Games Cost Sell price B W B2 W2 N/A N/A * X Y OR AS N/A $ 10,000 S M US UM Sw Sh S V ZA N/A $ 20,000 In the Generation V games, the Big Nugget can be sold to the ore maniac inside the Icirrus City Pokémon Center for $ 30000. In Pokémon Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon , the player can purchase a Big Nugget from Office Worker Royce for $ 5,000 in Po Town daily. Effect [ edit | edit source ] The Big Nugget serves no practical purpose other than to be sold. sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] This section contains material that may not be suitable for young readers. Viewer discretion is advised. Similarly to the Nugget 's Japanese name きんのたま kin no tama ("golden ball") being a slang term for testicles in Japanese; the Big Nugget's Japanese name adds でかい dekai ; literally making it a gigantic testicle. Starting from Pokémon Sword and Shield , the Big Nugget is tied with the Iron Ball for granting Fling its highest possible base power .
